checks.a_analysis: I have carefully compared the source document with the output JSON. Every paragraph, heading, and list item in the output can be directly mapped to the source document. No fabricated content or sections were introduced by the pipeline.
The source document does not contain any specific phone numbers, email addresses, or URLs. The output JSON correctly reflects this absence and does not hallucinate any contact data.

The source document is a single-page agenda. All elements, including the logo, the names of the Governor and Secretary, the meeting titles, date/time, and the full list of agenda items (including sub-committees), are present in the output JSON.
The source document does not contain any tables.

checks.a_analysis: All text content within the sections is preserved. The nested list under the sub-committee item is fully captured, as are the introductory text elements at the top of the page.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text in the output JSON matches the source document's text exactly. There are no alterations in numbers, dates, or wording that change the meaning of the content.

checks.a_analysis: I have thoroughly compared the source PDF with the provided JSON output. Each text block found in the source document (e.g., meeting titles, names of officials, agenda items) appears exactly once in the JSON output. There are no duplicated paragraphs, repeated sections, or list items appearing as text elsewhere.

The heading hierarchy in the JSON output follows a logical sequence. The main titles of the document ('North Carolina Longitudinal Data System' and 'Data Quality Committee Meeting') are assigned H2 levels, followed by 'AGENDA' at H3. While an H1 is omitted, the hierarchy does not skip levels (e.g., H2 to H4) and provides a clear structure for navigation. This adjustment is acceptable as normalized accessibility formatting.
The source document does not contain any tables. Correspondingly, the JSON output contains no table elements. There is no structural corruption to evaluate.

The reading order in the JSON output follows a top-to-bottom, left-to-right flow that captures all elements in their logical positions. It begins with the logo, followed by the leadership information located in the top header, the main meeting titles, and finally the agenda list. This sequence matches the intended flow of information in the source PDF.

The source document contains an agenda presented as an unordered bulleted list, with one item ('Revised: Next Steps for Each Sub-Committee') containing a nested sub-list. The JSON output correctly identifies this structure as an 'unordered' list and preserves the nesting of the sub-items (Lexicon, Common Elements, etc.) within the appropriate parent list item.
The source document contains no visible URLs or hyperlinks. The JSON output correctly reflects this by containing no link or anchor tags. There is no evidence of fabrication or missing links.

checks.a_analysis: The visual organization of the HTML document follows the logical flow of the original PDF. The content is presented in a single-column layout appropriate for web rendering, moving from the header information to the agenda list. While some elements (like the Governor's name) have been moved from the right to the left/center, and a table of contents has been added, the overall organization of information remains consistent and findable.
There are no tables present in the source PDF or the rendered HTML output.
The NCDIT logo from the source PDF is present in the HTML output. No other meaningful images or charts are present in the source document.

There is significant visual duplication of content in the HTML output. The names 'Roy Cooper Governor' and 'James A. Weaver Secretary and State Chief Information Officer' appear twice in the header section: once to the right of the NCDIT logo and once immediately below it on the left. Additionally, the meeting title 'North Carolina Longitudinal Data System Data Quality Committee Meeting' appears at the very top of the HTML document as a header and is then repeated in the body text after the names.
checks.b_evidence_source: "Roy Cooper Governor", "James A. Weaver Secretary and State Chief Information Officer", "North Carolina Longitudinal Data System", and "Data Quality Committee Meeting" each appear only once on the PDF page.
checks.b_evidence_output: The text "Roy Cooper Governor" and "James A. Weaver Secretary and State Chief Information Officer" is rendered twice in the header area. The document title is also rendered twice (at the very top and in the body).
checks.b_evidence_location: Top header area of the HTML document.
